name: Barri Office
description_short: Barri is a money services company offering check cashing, money transfers, bill payment, mobile reloads, and a prepaid Visa debit card with 30+ years of experience.
description_long:
Barri is an established money services provider with over 30 years of experience in the remittance and financial services space. The company operates a network of physical storefronts alongside a mobile app, positioning itself as a comprehensive financial services platform for underbanked and immigrant communities, particularly those sending money to Latin America and the Caribbean.

Barri's core services include check cashing with extended hours and transparent pricing, international and domestic money transfers available online, via app, or in-store, utility and service bill payments, mobile phone reloads across the US and Latin America, and a Visa prepaid debit card for account management and transfers. The company has partnered with Oportun to offer personal loans ranging from $300 to $10,000 for customers building credit or covering unexpected expenses. All primary services are accessible through their mobile app for iOS and Android users.

Barri differentiates itself through multi-channel accessibility (in-store, online, mobile app), extended operating hours for check cashing, partnership with established lending platforms, and a strong focus on serving Spanish-speaking communities with localized service. The company emphasizes ease of use and customer service responsiveness, with verified app reviews highlighting reliable performance and responsive support.

Barri functions primarily as a check-cashing and money services provider rather than a full-service bank or lender. While the Oportun partnership provides personal loan access, the company itself does not originate credit products. Users seeking comprehensive banking features like checking accounts or high credit limits should explore alternatives, though Barri's prepaid card and money movement services serve as functional tools for managing finances without traditional banking relationships.
address: 1224 N Flores St, San Antonio, TX 78212

company_info.headquarters: 1224 N Flores St, San Antonio, TX 78212

pros: 30+ years of operational experience in money transfer and remittance services
Multi-channel access: physical stores, website, and mobile app for transactions
Extended hours for check cashing with transparent, competitive pricing
International money transfer capability to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Partnership with Oportun enables personal loans from $300-$10,000 for credit building
High customer satisfaction: 4.9-star rating with verified reviews across iOS and Android
Mobile reloads available across US and Latin America for instant phone top-ups
cons: No traditional checking or savings accounts offered; limited banking features compared to banks or credit unions
Prepaid debit card limits account management compared to full-service bank accounts
Personal loans are through third-party partnership (Oportun), not directly offered by Barri
Physical store network may not be convenient for all customers despite multi-channel approach
Limited information on fee structure for check cashing and money transfer services on homepage
best_for: Immigrants and migrant workers regularly sending money to Latin America or the Caribbean
Unbanked and underbanked individuals without access to traditional checking accounts
Spanish-speaking communities seeking familiar, culturally-aligned financial services
Individuals building credit history who can access Oportun loans through Barri partnership
diagnosis: Barri is best for immigrants and underbanked individuals who need reliable check cashing, international money transfers, and basic money movement services. The main caveat is that Barri functions as a money services provider, not a full bank—it lacks checking accounts, savings features, and direct credit products, making it a complementary rather than primary financial institution.
services: Domestic money transfers (online, app, or in-store)
International money transfers to Latin America and Caribbean
Bill payment for utilities and services
Mobile phone reloads across US and Latin America
Mobile app for remote account management
Physical store locations for in-person transactions
